description: The mid-90s battle between Netscape's open-web vision and Microsoft's proprietary IE integration into Windows. The event tracks the shift in developer sentiment as Microsoft's bundling strategy dominated market share by 1998.

key_events.description: Netscape IPO signals dominance of open-web standards.
Microsoft releases IE 3.0, bundled for free with Windows 95.
DOJ files antitrust lawsuit against Microsoft for bundling.
IE becomes the dominant browser worldwide, crushing Netscape's business model.

calibration_notes: Measures the tipping point where convenience and distribution outweigh ideological support for open systems.
